summary: Large matching/decompilation pass for src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0A01, replacing many placeholder stubs with C and tightening several header prototypes. The decomp.dev report recorded +7324 matched bytes overall, 11 new 100% matches, and 25 improved unmatched items in ftCo_0A01, with one small regression for ftCo_800A2718.
what_changed: Added includes needed by the newly decompiled code: cm/camera.h, gr/grcastle.h, gr/grgreatbay.h, gr/grrcruise.h, and lb/lbvector.h.

Replaced placeholder comments such as /// #ftCo_800A229C, /// #ftCo_800A2C80, /// #ftCo_800A3908, /// #ftCo_800A4038, /// #ftCo_800A4768, /// #ftCo_800A4BEC, and /// #ftCo_800A4E8C with C implementations.
Added many later ftCo_0A01 implementations related to CPU/fighter behavior, including functions in the ranges ftCo_800A6700 through ftCo_800B33B0.

Implemented stage-aware logic using stage_info.internal_stage_id and stage-specific helpers for GREATBAY, CASTLE, ZEBES, RCRUISE, BIGBLUE, ICEMTN, STORY, ONETT, INISHIE1, FOURSIDE, Corneria, Venom, and related ground/platform checks.
Implemented collision and blast-zone tests with Stage_GetBlastZone* offsets, mpCheckFloor, mpCheckLeftWall, mpCheckRightWall, grLib_801C9E60, Camera_8003118C, and lbVector_Normalize.
Reworked item/island checks around HSD_GObj_Entities->items, GET_ITEM, it_8026C1B4, ftCo_800A5944, mpIsland_8005AB54, and mpIsland_8005AC8C.

Added matching-control helper wrappers named with _noinline, _noinline2, and _dontinline suffixes around functions such as ftCo_800A1B38, ftCo_800A1F3C, ftCo_IsGrabbing, ftCo_800A5980, ftCo_800A8DE4, ftCo_800AC30C, and ftCo_800B1DA0.

Updated ftCo_0A01.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or imprecise bool signatures to typed signatures such as s32 ftCo_800A229C(Fighter*, Vec3*), void ftCo_800AEA8C(Fighter*), void ftCo_800AEFB8(Fighter*), s32 ftCo_800A2C80(Fighter*), s32 ftCo_800A4768(Fighter*, Vec3*), s32 ftCo_800A6A98(Fighter*, Vec3*), s32 ftCo_800A6D2C(Fighter*, Vec3*), bool ftCo_800A6FC4(Fighter*, Vec3*, Vec3*), void ftCo_800A8210(Fighter*, Vec3*), and void ftCo_800A866C(Fighter*).
smart_moves: Kept temporary variable and stack-sensitive naming patterns such as sp2C, sp20, sp18, sp1C, var_r0, line_id, flags, and data where they helped preserve codegen while still making the control flow more readable.
Used small noinline/dontinline forwarding wrappers to influence call/inlining behavior without changing the semantic body of the target functions.

Converted old m2c-style gotos for item iteration and stage selection into structured for loops and switch statements where possible, while retaining explicit block labels/gotos in places where they likely helped match original branch layout.

Localized repeated blast-zone bounds checks around Fighter_x1A88 dimensions such as half_width and half_height, preserving the AI recovery/collision behavior while making the decompiled logic understandable.

Identified and added the real subsystem dependencies instead of leaving placeholder includes, which made the new implementations use typed camera, ground, stage, vector, and stage-specific declarations.
decomp_lessons: For ftCommon CPU/AI code, matching often depends on stage-specific helper calls and exact stage enum cases; do not flatten these into generic logic too early.
When converting m2c output, structured loops and switches can still match if branch-sensitive labels are retained only where needed.
Small forwarding wrappers with _noinline or _dontinline suffixes are a practical matching tactic when the original binary expects a separate call boundary or a particular inlining decision.

Header prototype cleanup should be done alongside implementation work; replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete Fighter*, Vec3*, s32, bool, and void signatures unlocks cleaner downstream decompilation.

AI movement/recovery code mixes fighter physics, collision rays, blast-zone limits, camera checks, item/platform state, and CPU command emission; future work should trace all of those dependencies before naming behavior.

Automated match reports are useful but not absolute: this PR gained many new matches while ftCo_800A2718 regressed slightly, so large matching passes should track both net bytes and per-function regressions.
naming_conventions: Wrapper functions used explicit matching-oriented suffixes: _noinline, _noinline2, _dontinline, _dontinline2, and _dontinline3.
Local inline helpers used range-local names such as inlineL0, inlineL1, inlineG0, inlineH0, and inlineH1 when semantic names were not yet evidenced.
New helper names retained address-based ftCo_XXXXXXXX identifiers where no meaningful symbol name was established.

Header prototypes preserved static address comments such as /* 09F198 */ and used concrete parameter names only when evidenced by the implementation, e.g. Fighter* fp, Vec3* arg1, Fighter* fp0, Fighter* fp1.
Unidentified Fighter_x1A88 fields remained offset-style names such as x1A88, x54, x60, x7C, x558, x564, and x568 rather than speculative semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added noinline forwarding calls around ftCo_800A1B38, ftCo_800A1F3C, ftCo_IsGrabbing, ftCo_800A5980, ftCo_800A8DE4, ftCo_800AC30C, and ftCo_800B1DA0 to preserve expected call boundaries.
Used inline wrappers such as ftCo_800A1C44_dontinline and ftCo_800A2718_dontinline chains to tune inlining behavior.
Retained explicit goto labels like block_18, block_23, and block_43 inside switch-heavy stage logic where direct structured control flow likely did not produce the desired assembly.
Preserved exact float constants and comparisons such as 0.00001f, 0.4f, 0.2f, 5.0f, -35.0f, -1.0471975430846214, and angle thresholds in CPU command logic.
Used PAD_STACK in later CPU command functions, indicating stack-shape adjustment was part of the matching strategy.
Kept m2c-like temporary names for stack Vec3s and scalar temporaries where register/stack allocation sensitivity was likely important.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported 11 new matches, 25 improvements in unmatched items, and one regression: ftCo_800A2718 decreased by 22 bytes, from 82.34% to 77.32%.
follow_up_queries: Inspect ftCo_800A2718 after PR 2524 to recover the small match regression introduced by the inlineL0/inlineL1 rewrite.
Search later PRs for semantic renames of Fighter_x1A88 fields used in ftCo_0A01 CPU recovery logic.
Find follow-up PRs that complete unmatched items improved here, especially ftCo_800B2AFC, ftCo_800ADE48, ftCo_800A3908, ftCo_800A4038, ftCo_800A6FC4, and ftCo_800ABBA8.
Compare the noinline/dontinline wrapper pattern from this PR against later cleanup PRs to see which wrappers were removed after better matching was found.
